prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the little brain inside your computer. It sits on that flat green board and does all the thinking work! It's pretty fast and can add numbers, remember things, and tell other parts of the computer what to do. Think of it as the conductor of an orchestra - it makes sure everything happens at just the right time. That's why when people say their computer is "slow," it's often because that little brain isn't working as fast as it used to.
